3.reason: Error: trailing space in format string may cause extra input consumption; should be "%d".

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  The format string includes a trailing space, which may cause issues with input parsing. It should be "%d" without the space.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  The format string includes a trailing space, which may cause issues with input parsing; it should be "%d" without the space.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: trailing space in format string may cause extra input consumption; should be "%d".
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  trailing space in format string may cause unexpected waiting for whitespace; should be "%d"
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  The trailing space in the format string causes scanf to wait for non-whitespace input, which can lead to incorrect reading or program hanging. It should be "%d".
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Trailing space in scanf format string causes it to wait for non-whitespace input, potentially blocking or misreading subsequent inputs.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [The format string has an unnecessary trailing space which can cause input parsing issues, especially when next input is read immediately. Also, the function should not read input here—it should receive the value to push as an argument.]

8.reason: Error: should store at a[j] (0-indexed) or adjust logic; storing at j+1 wastes index 0 and misaligns top pointer.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  The element should be stored at index j, not j+1, because j is the current top index (number of elements). This causes the stack to skip index 0 and misalign.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  The element should be stored at index j, not j+1, because j represents the current top index (next empty position). This causes the stack to skip index 0 and misalign.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: should store at a[j] (0-indexed) or adjust logic; storing at j+1 wastes index 0 and misaligns top pointer.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  index j+1 can exceed array bounds (e.g., j=99 gives index 100, but array size is 99); also array is 1-indexed but insufficient size
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Since j represents the current number of elements (or the index of the next free slot), the new element should be placed at a[j], not a[j+1].
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Off-by-one error: j represents the current number of elements, so the new element should be stored at index j, not j+1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  // Should be a[j] = news; because 'j' is the current number of elements (next available index). Using j+1 skips index 0 and causes out-of-bounds when j=99 (writes to a[100]).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [If j is the current count of elements (0-based count), then valid indices are 0 to j-1. Pushing at j+1 skips index j and may write beyond allocated array if j=98 -> index 99, but array is size 99 (indices 0-98).]

10.reason: The return value should be j+1 only when a push is successful, but it always returns j+1 even if the stack is full (error case), incorrectly updating the top index.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  The return value should be j+1 only when a push is successful, but it always returns j+1 even if the stack is full (error case), incorrectly updating the top index.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  The return value should be j+1 only when push succeeds, but it always returns j+1 even on error, which corrupts the stack index.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Logical error: unconditionally returns j+1 even when the stack is full and push fails, incorrectly incrementing the element count.

17.reason: When j is the count of elements in the stack, the top element is located at index j-1. It should print a[j-1].

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  The element to pop should be at index j-1 (the top element), not j, because j is the number of elements (next free index). This prints an uninitialized or wrong value.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Should print a[j-1] because j is the next empty index, so the top element is at j-1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  When j is the count of elements in the stack, the top element is located at index j-1. It should print a[j-1].
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Off-by-one error: when j is the count of elements, the top element is at index j-1, not j.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  // Should be a[j - 1] because the top element is at index j-1 when there are j elements. Accessing a[j] reads uninitialized memory or out-of-bounds.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Should print a[j - 1] if j is count of elements. But due to incorrect push storing at j+1, this accesses wrong/uninitialized memory.]

19.reason: The return value should be j-1 only when a pop is successful, but it always returns j-1 even if the stack is empty (error case), incorrectly updating the top index.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  The return value should be j-1 only when a pop is successful, but it always returns j-1 even if the stack is empty (error case), incorrectly updating the top index.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  The return value should be j-1 only when pop succeeds, but it always returns j-1 even on error, which corrupts the stack index.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Logical error: unconditionally returns j-1 even when the stack is empty and pop fails, incorrectly decrementing the element count.

23.reason: Error: array size should be 100 to match maximum capacity of 100 elements; currently only supports up to 98 elements due to 1-indexed storage.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  The stack size is 99, but the problem specifies a maximum capacity of 100. It should be int stack[100]; to match the requirement.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  The stack size is 99, but the problem specifies a maximum capacity of 100. It should be int stack[100];.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: array size should be 100 to match maximum capacity of 100 elements; currently only supports up to 98 elements due to 1-indexed storage.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  array size insufficient for capacity 100 with 1-based indexing; should be at least stack[101] (indices 1-100) or stack[100] with 0-based indexing
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  The problem specifies a maximum stack capacity of 100, so the array should be declared with size 100 to prevent out-of-bounds access.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Array size should be 100 to accommodate the maximum capacity of 100 elements as specified in the problem.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  // Stack size should be 100 to hold max 100 elements (indices 0-99). Declaring as 99 limits capacity to 99, causing premature "error" on 100th push.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Stack capacity should be 100 as per problem, but array is only size 99 → max 99 elements, violating spec. Also, indexing errors compound this.]

Also, the stack index logic is flawed: 'j' represents count of elements, so valid indices are 0 to j-1. 13.reason: // Condition should be j <= 0 because 'j' is the count of elements. If j==0, stack is empty. j-1<0 is equivalent to j<=0, but the logic for accessing element is wrong.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  // Condition should be j <= 0 because 'j' is the count of elements. If j==0, stack is empty. j-1<0 is equivalent to j<=0, but the logic for accessing element is wrong.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [If j is the number of elements, then top element is at index j-1. But condition checks j-1 < 0 → j < 1, which is correct for empty stack. However, in push, we stored at j+1, so indexing logic is inconsistent.]
